name: 25 · Prometheus Query
description: Demo of the Prometheus/PromQL executor backend. Start collects nothing; a single Prometheus `query` step binds the workspace `prometheus` resource `demo_prometheus` and runs the instant PromQL query `up` at the current evaluation time. `up` is the per-target scrape-health metric Prometheus exposes for every scrape job, so a stock `prom/prometheus` that self-scrapes always has at least one `up` series with no push step required. The step resolves the bound resource via ResourceChannel::ConfigOverlay — `base_url` (required), optional bearer `token`, optional `X-Scope-OrgID` `org_id`. Output is the fixed Prometheus envelope: `result_type` ("vector" | "matrix" | "scalar" | "string"), `series` (raw matrix result), `samples` (array of {metric, value, ts} for instant `vector` results), `sample_count`, `scalar` (raw scalar/string result), and `stats`. End maps `result_type`, `sample_count`, and `samples` onto the instance result. The `demo_prometheus` resource (`demos/resources/demo_prometheus.json`) points at a local, unauthenticated Prometheus at http://localhost:9090 (no token/org_id). Unlike the Loki demo there is no push step: stock `prom/prometheus` self-scrapes so the `up` metric is available immediately. A range-query secondary path would instead use `operation: query_range` with `since: 5m` and `step: 15s`, yielding a `matrix` result. Requires a `prometheus` resource named `demo_prometheus` in the workspace at /resources (auto-seeded from demos/resources/demo_prometheus.json).
